metadata.name: 2.5.d Percentage of persons declaring vegetables consumption 4-6 times a week
metadata.priority: Promotion of healthy lifestyle among adults and children
metadata.definition: Percentage of persons declaring vegetables consumption 4-6 times a week.
metadata.methodology: The indicator is calculated on the basis of information obtained from the European Health Interview Survey (EHIS).The EHIS is implemented by European Union Member States in accordance with recommendations issued by Eurostat regarding the thematic scope and the survey instruments applied. The survey is conducted periodically in EU countries and covers aspects such as health status, morbidity, mental health, accidents and injuries, use of health care services, preventive health care, and determinants of health. The survey results provide information on the current health status of the population and its determinants, in relation to demographic and socio-economic characteristics and place of residence.Within the determinants of health, data are collected, inter alia, on the frequency of fruit and vegetable consumption by both adults (aged 15 years and over) and children (aged over 6 months). Fruit consumption includes both raw and processed products. The same guidelines apply to vegetables, with the exception of potatoes, the consumption of which is not covered.
metadata.availability: Data provided at multi-year intervals; since 2010
metadata.notes: Data presented for 2010 relate to the survey carried out in 2009.
